mnemonics.title: Classical Planning Key Assumptions
mnemonics.content: FDS - Fully Determined Static world. Remember: F = Fully Observable, D = Deterministic, S = Static. F-D-S helps recall all three assumptions.

mnemonics.title: Planning Problem Components
mnemonics.content: I Got A Plan: Initial state, Goal state, Actions. Remember: I-G-A - Initial, Goal, Actions - the three essential elements.

mnemonics.title: Action Components
mnemonics.content: PEA - Preconditions and Effects make Actions work. Remember: P for Pre-conditions (before), E for Effects (after), A is the Action itself.

acronyms.usage: The three key assumptions of classical planning that define its scope and make the problem tractable.
A classic planning formalism that defines actions using preconditions, add-list (positive effects), and delete-list (negative effects).
The standard language for representing planning problems, based on STRIPS formalism.

visualTips.visualization: Think of each state as a complete snapshot/picture of the world at one moment. Like a chess board position - all pieces at their locations.
Imagine the state-space as a maze where each cell is a state. Actions move you between cells. Goal is finding the path to the exit.
Draw a cycle: Observe State → Formulate Plan → Execute Action → Update State → Repeat until Goal reached.

analogies.analogy: Like solving a Rubik's Cube with blindfolds removed - you can see the entire cube, each move has predictable results, and the cube doesn't change unless you move it.
Like having a complete map of a city rather than exploring it through a keyhole - you can see everything relevant to navigate.
Like a train on fixed tracks - pressing the button always moves it to the same next station, no chance of going elsewhere.
Like a puzzle on a table that only changes when you pick up and move pieces - nothing moves on its own.
